name: Worthington Federal Savings Bank, FSB
description_short: FDIC-insured federal savings bank established in 1935, offering deposit and loan products with local community focus and experienced staff.
description_long: Worthington Federal Savings Bank, FSB was established on March 1, 1935, as a Federal Savings & Loan Association chartered to help the Worthington community. The institution made its first loan of $800 on March 30, 1935, and has maintained continuous operations for nearly 90 years. In 1978, the bank expanded by acquiring the Lakefield Building & Loan Association. Despite technological modernization over the decades, the bank emphasizes maintaining the same friendly, knowledgeable service that characterized its early operations. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including online banking, deposit products, loan products, and other financial services. Customers can access account information remotely through internet banking services, order checks online, and utilize financial calculators. The institution provides both consumer deposit and lending services, with a stated commitment to serving the local region. Worthington Federal distinguishes itself through its deep historical roots in the Worthington community and surrounding areas, having financed local development for nearly a century. The bank continues to service all loans it has made, indicating long-term commitment to borrowers. Its status as an FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government provides deposit protection. The website provides limited specific details about current product offerings, interest rates, loan terms, or fee structures. Prospective customers would need to contact the bank directly or visit in person to obtain detailed pricing and product information. The institution appears to be a community-focused regional bank rather than a large national player.
services: Online banking and internet account access
Password and account management (Access ID enrollment)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. Government backing
Nearly 90-year operating history demonstrating stability and longevity
Continues to service all loans it has originated, indicating customer retention focus
Online banking and internet access to account information for convenience
Friendly, experienced staff emphasized in marketing materials
Deep community integration and local lending focus since 1935
Financial calculators available on website for customer planning
cons: Limited product and pricing information available on website
Appears to be regional institution with potentially limited branch network
No specific APR, interest rates, or fee information disclosed online
Website lacks details on loan approval process, requirements, or timelines
No online loan application process mentioned on available pages
best_for: Worthington, Ohio residents seeking community-focused banking relationships
Borrowers prioritizing long-term lender relationships and local decision-making
Customers in surrounding communities wanting established, historically stable institution
diagnosis: Best suited for Worthington-area residents and regional customers seeking relationship-based community banking with strong historical stability and FDIC protection. Primary caveat is limited online transparency regarding specific products, rates, and terms—direct contact with the bank is necessary for detailed financial information and application processes.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
